commits:
[{'sha': '477673cd23454e91fa5b4284bfeafd93ed462b42', 'message': 'Fixed #32737 -- Preserved requested language code in get_language_info().

Previously, get_language_info() returned the base language code (e.g.,
"de") when called with a country-specific variant (e.g., "de-ch") that
wasn't directly in LANG_INFO. This made it impossible to compare the
returned code against LANGUAGE_CODE in templates when using
country-specific language codes in the LANGUAGES setting.

The function now returns the originally requested language code in all
cases, including both the generic fallback path (e.g., "de-ch" -> "de")
and the explicit fallback path (e.g., "zh-my" -> "zh-hans").'}]

+class TestAsyncLoginLogoutAfterSyncMiddleware(TestCase):
+    @classmethod
+    def setUpTestData(cls):
+        cls.user = User.objects.create_user(
+            "test_user", "test@example.com", "test_password"
+        )
+        cls.user2 = User.objects.create_user(
+            "test_user2", "test2@example.com", "test_password2"
+        )
+
+    def setUp(self):
+        self.middleware = AuthenticationMiddleware(lambda req: HttpResponse())
+        self.client.force_login(self.user)
+        self.request = HttpRequest()
+        self.request.session = self.client.session
+        # Populate self.request.user.
+        self.middleware(self.request)
+        # .user is lazy, so materialize it by accessing an attribute.
+        self.request.user.is_authenticated
+
+    async def test_user_after_alogin(self):
+        await alogin(self.request, self.user2)
+        self.assertEqual(self.request.user, self.user2)
+
+    async def test_user_after_alogout(self):
+        await alogout(self.request)
+        self.assertEqual(self.request.user, AnonymousUser())",

Introduced ModelStateOptions class to mirror Options that are used
for Model in order to allow `Model` or `ModelState` to be provided
as inputs to this function.

Changed `database_forwards` and `database_backwards` functions to
only render the models after checking whether migration is allowed.'}]

Changed `database_forwards` and `database_backwards` functions to
only render the models after checking whether migration is allowed.'}]
review_comments: ['Interested in feedback on whether this (and the equivalent changes) are a possible performance hit / duplicated functionality until the need to actually have the model passed in to the schema function is removed.\n\nWas attempting to test locally to see if this change had any imprvoement on the run time for migrations that would always return false from `allow_migrate_model` (see closed ticket here: https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/36992), but found the `state_forwards` model rendering (https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/27737) was still the culprit for the worst of the performance in my specific usecase.']

review:
[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "conclusion": "修复了BaseModelFormSet.get_queryset()中回退排序逻辑，确保稳定排序。",
    "description": "变更将回退排序逻辑从仅检查`qs.ordered`升级为检查`qs.totally_ordered`。当查询集未完全排序时，新的逻辑会尝试保留现有的排序字段（来自查询集、模型Meta或空列表），并附加主键（'pk'）以确保排序的稳定性和唯一性。然而，在获取`current_ordering`时，如果`qs.query.order_by`为`False`（表示显式地`order_by()`未调用），代码会将其视为空元组/列表。但在Django中，当未指定排序时，`qs.query.order_by`是一个空元组`()`，而不是`False`。虽然`or`操作符能正确处理空元组（视为Falsey），但变量名`current_ordering`可能引起误解，且逻辑依赖于`or`的短路求值，可读性稍差。此外，当`current_ordering`为空时，`qs.order_by(*current_ordering, \"pk\")`等价于`qs.order_by(\"pk\")`，这是正确的。",
    "evidence": "if not qs.totally_ordered:\n    current_ordering = qs.query.order_by or qs.model._meta.ordering or []\n    qs = qs.order_by(*current_ordering, \"pk\")",
    "advice": "建议将`current_ordering`的赋值逻辑写得更明确，例如使用三元表达式或条件判断，以提高可读性。例如：`current_ordering = qs.query.order_by if qs.query.order_by else (qs.model._meta.ordering or ())`。同时，考虑添加注释说明`qs.query.order_by`在未排序时为`()`。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Rightness",
    "conclusion": "新增了四个测试用例，覆盖了get_queryset()回退排序的各种场景。",
    "description": "测试用例分别验证了：1) 当模型无Meta.ordering且查询集无显式排序时，回退到按'pk'排序；2) 当查询集有显式非唯一字段排序时，会附加'pk'；3) 当模型有Meta.ordering但非唯一时，会附加'pk'；4) 当查询集已按'pk'排序（完全排序）时，不会重复添加'pk'。这些测试全面覆盖了变更逻辑，确保了功能的正确性。",
    "evidence": "",
    "advice": "",
    "confidence": 1.0,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]
commits: [{'sha': '83065aed4cf60d3f87e53fbba399c1309417e987', 'message': 'Refs #37004 -- Added coverage for BaseModelFormSet.get_queryset() fallback ordering.'}, {'sha': '5a1f7691b323df4a7295eaba9b56aa9e72587063', 'message': 'Fixed #37004 -- Used QuerySet.totally_ordered in BaseModelFormSet.get_queryset() for stable ordering.'}]
review_comments:
['Django's [style guide](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/6.0/internals/contributing/writing-code/coding-style/#:~:text=assertIs%28%E2%80%A6%2C%20True/False%29%20for%20testing) suggests preferring assertIs over assertTrue (with the possible exception of asserting over values produced from python's stdlib, although opinions differ on this).

But while here, I'm realizing this is here just for "self-documentation", no?

In that case, I would not assert the final status of the queryset property, but perhaps the initial status of the queryset property, so that you are self-documenting why the "pk" needs to be appended (or not). (All of the test cases show that "pk" is appended to the ordering, so by asserting that's it's `totally_ordered`, we're kind of re-testing that property instead of unit testing your changes.)', "This test adds coverage for the existing feature (which was merged without a test). I would organize your PR with two commits: one commit (first) that is `Refs #37004 -- Added coverage for ...` containing only this test, and then a second commit `Fixed #37004 -- ...` for the rest.

Your last test also passes without your changes, but I see that it's mostly here to check an edge case in your changes, so I don't really mind having it in either commit.", 'Yes last test is a bit to self-documentation too, but a bit on the overkill side :P 
Please let me know if I didn't get your suggestions right regarding the commits. I'm still struggling a bit here especially because the checklist mentions "the commit" in singular.', 'Thanks! Makes a lot of sense.', "Commits look great. Last needed tweak: we want the test to pass when I check out your first commit. It fails like this because of the rename:

```py
AssertionError: Tuples differ: ('id',) != ('pk',)

First differing element 0:
'id'
'pk'

- ('id',)
+ ('pk',)
```

So we need to adjust both commits.", 'determisnistic -> deterministic', "Done thanks. I didn't know this approach for commits, really interesting.", "Yeah, let's say we merge a nasty bug here. We can revert your second commit while keeping the coverage you added without any change.", 'A lot of people bisect Django to find behavior changes, so we try to leave the tests passing at every commit.', "Thanks @rodbv 👍 -- nice tests!

Some cosmetic comments for you on how to organize the commits. If not self-evident, I'll offer that when doing this, I find it helpful to do `git reset --soft HEAD~n`.

Give some thought to the PR title/commit msg also; should be phrased in past tense to  blend in.", 'Looks great!', "> Thanks @rodbv 👍 -- nice tests!
> 
> Some cosmetic comments for you on how to organize the commits. If not self-evident, I'll offer that when doing this, I find it helpful to do `git reset --soft HEAD~n`.
> 
> Give some thought to the PR title/commit msg also; should be phrased in past tense to blend in.

Thanks for the tip using `git reset --soft HEAD~n`, I didn't know this, I was about to do the hard way with rebase :D ", 'Welcome aboard! ⛵ ', '> Welcome aboard! ⛵ 

\U0001f979 Thanks! ']
